evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: 增长 (growth)
entities.how_described: 本集围绕增长为什么会放缓或停滞展开,指出面对停滞不要盲目加快脚步,而是按顺序问四个诊断问题(流失、定价、留存、渠道),并最终反问自己:你真的需要增长吗?

evidence.quote: There's a series of questions that I asked to diagnose why is growth slowing.

evidence.quote: Because you're going to get eaten alive if you're, especially... And just to be clear, this is B2B SaaS primarily that we're talking about here.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: CAC
entities.how_described: 本集在重新定位定价话术时提到,如果保持相同的客户获取成本,能让获客线索翻倍,客户就愿意为同一产品支付更多费用。

evidence.quote: At the same ROI as we had before, same CAC, I doubled leads."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: WP Engine
entities.how_described: 本集说它是嘉宾 Jason Cohen 创立的公司之一,也是一家独角兽,通过代理商(而非仅靠直销)销售其 WordPress 网站是其获客渠道的重要部分。

evidence.quote: Jason is a four-time founder, including two unicorns, one being WP Engine.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Same thing happens with my company, WP Engine.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: ... 2017 for Central Texas, which I co-won with the CEO of WP Engine Heather Brunner, which is awesome because I often call Heather a late joining co-founder, because that's what ... LinkedIn, that's what Reid Hoffman called Jeff Wiener, because Jeff was four years in, but was so impactful to everything, the success of the company, the culture, the da, da, da, da, that basically is a co-founder.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And that's exactly what Heather is like at WP Engine.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And I used to say to people at WP Engine, like, "If I just told you that Heather was a co-founder, you'd say, 'Yeah, no kidding.'"
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: HubSpot
entities.how_described: 本集提到它曾测试通过代理商而非直销来卖产品,这最终占了其 50% 的营收,是其能够继续增长的主要原因之一。

evidence.quote: For example, HubSpot famously tested selling through agencies instead of direct.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: SmartBear
entities.how_described: 本集提到它是嘉宾 Jason Cohen 创办的一家公司,他在这里分享了让客户在取消时填写下拉菜单收集流失原因其实充满噪音的故事。

evidence.quote: I was like, "Count the number of blog posts on a smartbear.com."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So at SmartBear, people would cancel, we'd put up this form and a dropdown list, too expensive, project ended, this little stuff like we do, so we could gather data.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: Buffer
entities.how_described: 本集把它作为例子,说它完全可以走向高端市场,但出于公司文化认同(致力于服务小人物)而主动选择不去转型做企业业务。

evidence.quote: We are a company... Like Buffer's a great example.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Buffer could go up-market and try to sell social media tools to whatever, but they realized, "We are a company for the little people.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: Constant Contact
entities.how_described: 本集说它面对增长停滞时,亲自去各大城市举办线下研讨会教小企业怎么做邮件营销,借此成功重启了增长。

evidence.quote: So an example of getting creative on a channel is what Constant Contact did when they had this very problem of, "Growth is slow.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: So the restaurateur and the dentist and everyone would come to these sessions and they'd teach them how, of course, teaching them how with Constant Contact so they became customers, right?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: Groove
entities.how_described: 本集说这家公司把客户取消邮件里的问法从「你为什么取消?」改成「是什么让你取消?」,使有效回复率从 10% 翻倍到了 20%。

evidence.quote: And I stole this from a company called Groove, who has this great case study online about this very thing.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: Hidden Multipliers
entities.how_described: 本集提到这是嘉宾即将出版的第一本实体书,讲的是那些能对收入或利润产生巨大影响的小决策与小事(即乘数效应)。

evidence.quote: It's called Hidden Multipliers.

evidence.quote: So the book is called Hidden Multipliers, and you can pre-order it at hiddenmultipliers.com.

evidence.quote: And there's an S at the end, Hidden Multipliers.

evidence.quote: But I think Hidden Multipliers is certainly my best work.
